project-aggregation.label: Summarize Data for Insights
project-aggregation.summary: Use aggregation and grouping to answer business questions.

quiz-sql-agg-query.title: Write a SQL query to find the average price of products in each category from the products_catalog dataset. Show the category and the average price as avg_price.

quiz-sql-count-query.title: Write a SQL query to count how many products are in each category in the products_catalog dataset. Show the category and the count as product_count.

sketch-aggregation-intro.title: Why Aggregate Data?
sketch-aggregation-intro.bodyMarkdown: Aggregation helps you turn raw data into meaningful summaries. For example, you might want to know the total sales per product, the average price in a category, or the number of customers in each city. SQL provides functions like `SUM`, `AVG`, `COUNT`, `MIN`, and `MAX` to help you answer these questions efficiently.

sketch-group-by.title: Grouping with GROUP BY
sketch-group-by.bodyMarkdown: The `GROUP BY` clause lets you organize data into groups based on one or more columns. When combined with aggregation functions, you can summarize each group. For example, grouping sales by product or employees by department.
